Comprehension Excerpts on LaaS/Homocracy — Volume 1

Comprehension Excerpts on LaaS/Homocracy — Volume 1

  1. Effect Lasting Change



🌍 To Reformers & Change‑Makers

💭 Most people who want change were shaped by life — injustice, loss, or moments that forced us to question the world around us. Sometimes the pain is so deep you just want to cry out and be heard.

⚖️ With time, one truth becomes clear: lasting reform doesn’t come from replacing individuals in power — it comes from building strong, fair, and enduring systems.

✨ Good has always faced resistance, yet history shows it ultimately prevails. Whether you call it God, the Creator, the Starter of the Universe, or simply Truth — there is a higher order that favors justice, balance, and restoration.

🧱 Real change takes patience. It may mean laying foundations others will finish. That is not failure — that is legacy.

📘 I consider myself a reformer. For the past 10 years, I’ve been studying and building toward a social order designed to last — not to be discarded by history, but to serve humanity with clarity and fairness.

🤝 If this resonates with you, reach out: onkezabahizi@gmail.com
🔍 Or search: Laastras

🌱 Let’s build with purpose — quietly, carefully, for ourselves and for generations to come.



  1. LaaS — Morshux — Porshux



🧭 Leadership as a Service (LaaS)

💡 LaaS starts from a simple truth: the main resource every human truly owns is leadership.

🌍 The other resources we depend on for life — land, water, energy, nature — are given. They belong to Nature, or what some call the Starter of the Universe (God). They exist to support human life, and all humans should have access to them.

⚖️ LaaS means that the only legitimate means of exchange is leadership — applied through responsible roles in managing the resources that sustain human existence (Morshux).

🔁 In return for this service, each person receives their fair **portion of life‑supporting resources (Porshux). This model of trade is called Services Exchange (SerX):
• 🛠️ Service rendered → Leadership (LaaS)
• 🎁 Remuneration → Porshux

🏛️ To create lasting change, LaaS must operate through SerX, supported by a system of governance called Homocracy —
✨ Governance by Equals.



  1. Homocracy — Idealism or Necessity



⚖️ Homocracy — Governance by Equals

Is Homocracy just idealism… or is it a necessity of our time?

🌍 Across the world, systems built on hierarchy and concentration of power keep failing the many while serving the few. We replace leaders, yet the problems remain.

🧩 Homocracy asks a different question: What if governance was shared? What if leadership was a service, not a privilege — and responsibility was distributed among equals?

🔁 Equality in governance is not about sameness. It’s about shared accountability, clear roles, and systems strong enough to outlive individuals.

⏳ As societies grow more complex, centralized control becomes fragile. Governance by equals may no longer be a dream — it may be the only sustainable path forward.

💬 What do you think?
👉 Is Homocracy an idealistic vision, or a practical necessity for lasting reform?





  1. Can Equality Coexist with Efficiency?



💬 Discussion

⚖️ Can equality in governance coexist with efficiency?

Many believe shared governance slows decision‑making. Others argue that concentrated power is fast — but fragile.

🧩 Homocracy asks: What if equality is not the enemy of efficiency, but its foundation? Clear roles, shared responsibility, and strong systems — not strong individuals.

⏱️💡 What’s your view?
👉 Does governance by equals make societies stronger or slower?





  1. Where to Start LaaS



⚖️ How could Homocracy or LaaS work in your country or community?

At its core, Homocracy / Leadership as a Service (LaaS) is about equally sharing the resources that support human existence — using digital public infrastructure to ensure fairness, access, and accountability.

🧩 Think essentials: land, water, minerals, energy, services, identity, data.
💻 Think systems: digital identity, transparent roles, service-based exchange.

🌱 Where should this begin where you live?
👉 At the local council? community projects? public services? digital platforms?

💬 How would you apply Homocracy or LaaS — and where should the first step be?





  1. We Only Change Faces in Power



🧭 Why Replacing Leaders Rarely Fixes Broken Societies

🗳️ In most democracies, when things go wrong, we do one thing: replace leaders. Elections come and go, faces change — yet inequality, corruption, and inefficiency remain.

⚠️ The flaw is not always the individual. The flaw is the system.

Democracy often focuses on who governs rather than how governance works. Without resilient, fair, and equality‑centered systems, new leaders are quickly absorbed — or broken — by the same structures that failed before.

🧱 Lasting change requires systems that:

  • share responsibility

  • distribute power fairly

  • outlive individuals

✨ This is why democracy must be upgraded, not discarded.

⚖️ Leadership as a Service (LaaS) reframes leadership as a responsibility, not a privilege.
🏛️ Homocracy — Governance by Equals operationalizes this by building systems where power, accountability, and resources are shared.

🔍 Curious to learn more? Research Laastras.

💬 After exploring, what’s your view?
👉 Can Homocracy and LaaS fix what leader‑replacement alone cannot?





  1. Reforms that Last



📜 A Historical Reform That Lasted — and Why It Worked

🏛️ One of the most durable reforms in history was the creation of the constitutional system of governance, beginning with documents like the Magna Carta (1215) and later evolving into constitutional frameworks.

⚠️ This reform did not succeed because a better king was chosen. It worked because power itself was constrained by rules. Authority was redistributed, roles were defined, and governance became a system — not the will of one individual.

🧱 The key lesson: lasting reform happens when systems change, not just leaders.

✨ Leadership as a Service (LaaS) follows this same principle. Leadership is treated as a responsibility to serve, not a privilege to rule.
🏛️ Homocracy — Governance by Equals operationalizes this idea by designing systems where power, accountability, and resources are shared.

🔒 The goal is not endless reform cycles, but a constitution so fair and balanced it becomes immutable — capable of lasting across generations.

💬 What do you think?
👉 Which systems today need reform more than their leaders?





  1. TAToE and SCoPS Can Lead to Permanent Leaders



⚖️ Should leadership be temporary, rotational, or permanent?

📜 History gives us a mixed answer. Some leaders brought such noble and transformative change that we wish they had stayed longer. Others overstayed and caused harm. Each leadership change resets momentum, ideas, and energy — sometimes for the better, sometimes for the worse.

🧭 But what if leadership didn’t have to carry so much risk?

✨ Consider an absolute principle we can call TaToE — The Absolute Truth of Existence:

“Existence is governed by free will and mutual respect, not by imposed authority.”

🔐 If free will must be protected, then every human need SCoPS — Self‑Contained Personal Space.
This means each person is guaranteed their Porshux — their portion of resources that support human existence — everything needed to live with dignity.

🧱 If a constitution immutably guarantees SCoPS, individuals are protected regardless of who leads or what leaders decide.

➡️ In that case, leadership terms become less critical. Leadership could even be permanent, because power no longer threatens autonomy.

⚙️ This is the essence of LaaS — Leadership as a Service, operationalized through Homocracy — Governance by Equals: systems protect people, not personalities.

💬 What do you think?
👉 Should leadership be temporary, rotational, or permanent?





  1. Major Flaw in Constitutions



🧠 What Is Broken in All Modern‑Day Constitutions?

Most modern constitutions are beautifully written — yet they miss the most important question of all:

👉 Why do we need governance in the first place?

Governance is not sacred. Power is not divine. Authority is not self‑justifying.

The only reason governance exists is simple and unavoidable:

🌍 To exist, humans rely on resources that must be shared.

If resources were unlimited and private, each of us would live however we pleased, without caring what others think or do. No laws. No states. No politics.

But shared resources create interdependence 🤝 — and interdependence creates trade 🔄.

⚖️ What modern constitutions fail to state clearly is this:

Governance should exist for one purpose only:
to equally distribute the resources that sustain human life, and to establish trade rules that protect that equality while enabling fair exchange of goods and services.

Everything else — power struggles, ideologies, elite privilege — is noise.

🔧 Until constitutions are rebuilt around this core truth, governance will continue to serve itself instead of humanity.

It’s time to fix the foundation.





  1. Fix the Trade System



🔧 Fixing Trade Fixes Everything

Trade is the outermost container of all human relationships.

Every interaction we have — social, political, cultural, even moral — eventually passes through trade. How we access resources. How we exchange value. How we survive together.

When trade is broken, everything breaks:
⚔️ war | 💥 conflict | 📉 poverty | 🚫 unemployment | ⚖️ inequality

That’s why these problems cannot be solved by ideology, charity, or force.
They can only be solved by fixing the trade system itself.

🧭 A just trade system must rest on one absolute principle:

All resources that support human existence must be equally shared.

From this principle emerge two essential layers:

🔁 Services Exchange (SerX)
Each individual holds one role in the collective resources management system — and receives in return a Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence (Porshux): everything needed to live with dignity.

💻 Virtual Currency (VirCu)
Money becomes pure value numbers living only in computers. No paper. No hoarding power. Just transparent exchange.

Together, SerX + VirCu form the core of LaaS — Leadership as a Service, giving rise to Homocracy — Governance by Equals.

🌍 Fix trade fairly, and society heals itself.

How about where you live? What system is most broken in your society today?





  1. Constitution Made Immutable



💭 A Personal Moment That Made Me Question Leadership & Governance

There was a moment — not unique to one country — that deeply unsettled me.

A Head of State, driven by ambition, decides to change the constitution to fit his goals. The justification sounds legal. Even patriotic.

But then reality hits.

👥 People take to the streets to oppose the move.
🔥 Protests turn into vandalism.
🚓 Police intervene.
🩸 Blood is shed.
🔒 Voices of dissent are imprisoned.

And suddenly, a document meant to protect society becomes the spark that tears it apart.

That moment forced a hard question:

What kind of constitution collapses the moment power desires more power?

The answer is uncomfortable but clear:

➡️ Any constitution that is not grounded in an absolute, immutable principle is vulnerable to abuse.

🧭 The Missing Foundation: TAToE

We need a constitution built on what I call TAToE — The Absolute Principle of Existence.

Existence is governed by free will and mutual respect, not by imposed authority.

From this principle flows a powerful guarantee:

🧍‍♂️🧍‍♀️ SCoPS — Self‑Contained Personal Space

Every human individual must permanently own a protected space made of:

✔️ Free will
✔️ Mutual respect
✔️ A Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence (Porshux) — everything one needs to live with dignity

🔐 The Key to an Immutable Constitution

Once a constitution absolutely protects free will and SCoPS, it becomes forever immutable.

✍️ Amendments can still happen — as long as they never violate:

  • Free will

  • SCoPS (Porshux)

Such amendments cause no harm.

In fact, under this system, even the same leaders could remain in power permanently — without conflict — because:

⚖️ Power no longer threatens existence
🕊️ Governance no longer violates dignity
🔥 Power‑struggle politics disappear

🌍 Maybe the problem was never who leads us.

💡 Maybe the problem is what we allow leadership to touch.

What personal moment made you question leadership or governance — and how can TAToE solve the problem?



  1. Pure Democracy



🌱 From Leadership as a Service to Homocracy

For centuries, governance has rested on a quiet assumption:

👑 Leadership is reserved for a privileged few.

Presidents, kings, elites, parties — the many are governed, the few decide.

Leadership as a Service (LaaS) breaks that assumption completely.

🧭 Leadership Reimagined

LaaS starts from a radical but simple idea:

Every human individual who has come of age is a leader.

Not someday. Not symbolically. Now.

In this sense, LaaS is Pure Democracy — because every individual in the demos is a leader, not a subject.

☁️ Delivering Leadership at Scale

Leadership cannot operate in chaos. It needs structure.

To deliver LaaS, we introduce a proxy system where individuals serve society through defined roles, and their work benefits everyone.

This cloud‑like layer is called:

🔄 Morshux

Management of Resources that Support Human Existence

Through Morshux:

  • Individuals contribute leadership through one role

  • The results of that role are propagated to the whole population

In return, the individual receives:

🎁 Porshux

Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence — everything one needs to live with dignity.

⚖️ From LaaS to Homocracy

Homocracy (pronounced hoʊˈmɒkrəsi) — and not LGBTQ terminology — is simply another name for Pure Democracy.

It means:

✔️ Everyone is a leader
✔️ Everyone is equal
✔️ No one holds power or privilege to subjugate others

Leadership becomes a service, not a throne.

💻 Why Now?

The Greek philosophers imagined democracy — but they lacked the tools to implement it fully.

Today, we have Digital Public Infrastructure.

With digital systems, transparency, and automation, LaaS becomes practical, scalable, and fair.

🌍 From distributed leadership emerges Homocracy:

Governance by Equals.

Not ruled.
Not represented.

Led — together.







  1. Curb Veto Power Abuse



🛑 Curb Veto Power Abuse

Veto power is often portrayed as dangerous — and history gives us good reason to worry.

Used poorly, a veto becomes a weapon:
❌ silencing dissent
❌ blocking progress
❌ entrenching power

But the problem is not veto power itself.

The problem is veto power without an absolute compass.

🧭 Veto Power Has a Place

In LaaS — Leadership as a Service and Homocracy — Governance by Equals, veto power does have a role.

✔️ It allows leaders to protect the constitution
✔️ It prevents rushed or harmful decisions
✔️ It preserves long-term societal stability

Within LaaS/Homocracy boundaries, veto power becomes a shield, not a sword.

🔐 The Immutable Core

What makes abuse impossible is the existence of an immutable part of the constitution — a permanent flight plan for leadership.

That immutable core consists of two non-negotiables:

🕊️ Full respect for free will
🧍‍♂️🧍‍♀️ SCoPS — Self-Contained Personal Space

SCoPS means every individual permanently owns:

  • their autonomy

  • their dignity

  • their Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence

Once these are declared untouchable, power loses its teeth.

⚓ Leadership Without Harm

With free will and SCoPS firmly protected:

⚖️ Leaders can veto decisions without abusing power
🌊 Society can progress or stand still without damage
🧠 Governance gains clarity and calm

That immutable core is to leadership what anchors and stabilizers are to a boat crossing stormy seas.

The boat may slow down.
It may change course.

But it will not capsize.

♻️ A Radical Consequence

One surprising outcome of LaaS/Homocracy:

Leaders can remain in power permanently — without abuse, stagnation, or lack of diversity.

Why?

Because when power cannot violate free will or SCoPS, it stops being dangerous.

🌍 Curb veto abuse — not by removing veto power, but by making harm structurally impossible.

That is governance by equals.



  1. Mutual Provision



🌍 When a Reform Truly Lasts

History shows us something powerful:

Real reform is not about changing faces at the top.
It is about changing the system beneath them.

Many democracies rotate leaders every few years — yet inequality, conflict, and instability remain.

Why?

Because replacing leaders is not the same as reforming the raison d’être of government.

🧭 The Reform That Endures

A reform lasts when it redefines why governance exists.

LaaS — Leadership as a Service (also called Homocracy — Governance by Equals) does exactly that.

It reforms trade.

It establishes mutual provision as the sole purpose of government.

Government exists for one reason only:

To ensure that every individual both contributes to and benefits from the resources that support human existence.

🧍‍♂️ The Protective Core: SCoPS

From this reform emerges a protected human space called:

🔐 SCoPS — Self-Contained Personal Space

Each person is guaranteed:

  • Their free will

  • Their dignity

  • Their fair access to the resources necessary to live

This protection stems from a deeper foundation:

🧭 TAToE — The Absolute Truth of Existence

Existence is governed by free will and mutual respect — not imposed authority.

When governance is anchored there, reform does not collapse with political turnover.

It stabilizes the system itself.

🚀 How We Implement It Without Political Crashes

To avoid direct confrontation with leaders in power, LaaS/Homocracy is being implemented through a private-sector start-up:

☁️ Laastras — Leadership as a Service Trade System

Rather than overthrowing systems, we build parallel, practical infrastructure.

Through Laastras, we reform trade first — because fixing trade fixes everything.

🤝 We Urgently Need Builders

We are looking for:

🕒 Volunteers who can dedicate at least 2 hours per week to help build traction
💻 Software engineers willing to contribute to core brands like:

  • Jobperon

  • GetClientele

If this vision resonates with you, reach out:

📩 onkezabahizi@gmail.com

True reform does not replace rulers.

It redefines the purpose of rule itself.

And that is how change lasts.





  1. Homocracy Is Realisable Through DPI



🌍 Homocracy & Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)

A Necessary Upgrade for Humanity

When Democracy was invented, there was no technological infrastructure capable of enabling real equality. No digital identity. No shared digital currency. No distributed storage. No real-time processing of collective resources.

Equality was an idea — not a system.

Today, that has changed. ⚡

With Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) we now have:

  • 🪪 Digital ID systems

  • 💱 Digital currency frameworks

  • ☁️ Cloud storage

  • 🖥️ Massive processing power

  • 🌐 Global connectivity

For the first time in history, we can technically build a system that shares resources fairly and efficiently.

So why are we still trapped in inequality?

Because we are still using an outdated governance model.

🔁 Upgrade Democracy → Homocracy

Homocracy = Governance by Equals
A practical system based on:

LaaS — Leadership as a Service

Leadership is no longer a position of power.
It becomes a service layer that coordinates resources for everyone’s benefit.

This is not idealism.

It is survival.

Without systemic equality, we continue suffering from:

  • ⚔️ War

  • 🍞 Poverty

  • 🧒 Malnutrition

  • 🚫 Unemployment

  • 📉 Structural inequality

⚙️ How It Works (Practically)

Imagine a national or global system that:

Input:
👥 All human capacity
🏗️ All material resources

Processing:
🧠 Digital coordination through DPI
📊 Intelligent allocation

Output:
🏠 Everything required to live with dignity

Or in one word:

Porshux

Your Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence

With DPI, such a system can be built in no time.

🚀 From Theory to Execution: Laastras

To prove Homocracy is not idealism but necessity, we designed Laastras — a start-up operationalizing this model through four brands:

  • 💼 Jobperon

  • 🤝 GetClientele

  1. World Trade System Consortium



🌍 Applying LaaS / Homocracy in Your Country or Community

A Two-Edged Strategy for Real Change

After long reflection, we concluded that LaaS — Leadership as a Service, through Homocracy — Governance by Equals, can be applied in two practical ways in any country or community.

This is not theory. It is implementation. ⚙️

1️⃣ Government Partnership Model

If a Head of State is convinced, he/she establishes a:

🏛️ SPLAASM

Special LaaS Ministry/Department

✔️ Independent
✔️ Outside executive control
✔️ Guardian of the LaaS/Homocracy Constitution
✔️ Provider of administrative services to Laastras
✔️ Investor securing national shares/stakes in Laastras

Each country’s SPLAASM would collectively form a new UN organ under the UN Secretary General:

🌐 WTSC

World Trade System Consortium

In this structure:

🔄 Laastras becomes the merger point between public and private sectors.
📈 It must remain directed permanently by its Founder & CEO, with succession rights (preferably within family lineage) to preserve vision continuity.

2️⃣ Investor-Ready Start‑Up Model

We will not create resistance movements. ❌
That is against our ethics and culture.

If governments decline to create SPLAASM or invest:

🚀 We continue building traction.
💼 We position Laastras as a bankable, investor-ready start-up.
🌍 We effect lasting systemic change from the private sector.

Until we become a global force for reckoning.

⚔️ The Two-Edged Strategy

Public sector pathway.
Private sector pathway.

Either way — LaaS/Homocracy advances.

  1. Efficiency in Homocracy



⚙️ How Can Efficiency Be Achieved in Equal‑Oriented Homocracy?

Many ask: If everyone is equal, won’t governance become slow and chaotic?

The answer is simple:

🚫 Without Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), yes — it would be slow and impractical.
✅ With DPI, it becomes efficient and scalable.

🌐 Equality Does NOT Mean Everyone Is a Ruler

In Homocracy, equality is not achieved by assuming everybody governs everything.

Democratic institutions like Parliament can remain.
What changes is the foundation.

Law does not control human behavior — people are entirely free.

Law is confined by:

🧭 TAToE — The Absolute Principle of Existence

Free will + Mutual respect
Not imposed authority.

This means law regulates only one thing:

🏛️ The Institutions That Produce SCoPS

📦 SCoPS — Self‑Contained Personal Space

Everything one needs to live with dignity.

Also known as:

🍞 Porshux — Portion of Resources that Support Human Existence

🔄 Where Does Equality Come From?

Equality means:

👤 Every person performs one role in

🛠️ Morshux — Management of Resources that Support Human Existence

And in return receives their SCoPS.

One role.
One contribution.
One dignified outcome.

That is efficiency.

🗳️ What About Voting?

With DPI:

  1. Instant identity verification

  2. Secure digital voting

  3. Real‑time transparency

  4. Efficient coordination

Participation becomes fast, accountable, and practical.

🚀 LaaS — Leadership as a Service

In LaaS, the principle behind Homocracy:

✨ Everyone is a leader — through their one role in Morshux.

Leadership is not domination.
It is coordinated contribution.

  1. Our Legacy



🌍 LaaS / Homocracy — For Ourselves and for the Next Generation

Different people have different priorities.
Different regimes promise different reforms.

Yet some needs are universal:

🎓 Education
🏥 Health
🛣️ Infrastructure
📦 Logistics

To please the masses, political activists and power figures focus on these visible needs.

And yes — progress happens.

But something deeper is often ignored.

⚖️ The Bigger Picture They Miss

No matter the reforms, most systems fail to address the root problem:

❗ Uneven Distribution of Resources

A country may be labeled “developed.”
GDP rises 📈
Standards of living improve (for some) 🏙️

Yet only a handful capture the lion’s share —
often at very high and unpleasant social cost.

We are then encouraged to admire them as models.

Examples frequently praised:

🇺🇸 United States
🇨🇳 Greater China
🇯🇵 The Miracle of Japan
🇿🇦 South Africa
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇦🇪 UAE
🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia
🇵🇰 Pakistan
🇮🇳 India

But behind the metrics:

💔 Broken families
⚔️ Violence and crime
⚖️ Injustices
🧠 Pressure-related diseases from disruptive competition
🏦 Capital monopoly by a powerful elite few

The legacy?

Developed — yet broken nations.

🛠️ A Different Legacy

For more than a decade, we have polished:

🚀 LaaS — Leadership as a Service

⚖️ Homocracy — Governance by Equals

We have tried to “leave no stone unturned.”

Is it perfect? No.
Will humanity still need superhuman intervention? Yes.

But we believe this system can resolve — to a very good degree —:

🕊️ Conflicts & war
💼 Unemployment
⚖️ Injustice
🍞 Poverty
📉 Inequality

We envision an era of:

✨ “Peace and Security” ✨

Leaving room for any superior being —
God, Truth, or Universe Starter —
To address what we cannot:

🌪️ Natural disasters
🧬 Incurable diseases
⏳ Old age
👽 Unknown external threats
🌑 Possible invisible dark influences

👣 For Us — and For Them

LaaS/Homocracy is not only for the next generation.
It is for us now.

It aims to leave a legacy that is:

✔ Developed
✔ Stable
✔ Fair
✔ Structurally balanced

If you want to understand how this system achieves that, read my recent posts.

  1. Change Is Possible



🌍 Why We Personally Believe Change Is Possible

Many ask us: Why are you so confident this can work?

Because we understand two things clearly.

⚖️ Policy Can Accelerate It

If LaaS — Leadership as a Service
through Homocracy — Governance by Equals
were adopted at policy level, the transition would be fast.

Structural alignment would move quickly.

But that is not where the real engine of change lives.

🚀 The Real Shift Must Happen in the Private Sector

True transformation depends on:

💼 Investors
🏗️ Entrepreneurs
💻 Technologists
🌱 Founders

They must see the necessity of joining:

🧠 AORI — Acquire Or Integrate

This is the hardest part.

Efficient knowledge management.
Strategic information dissemination.
Systematic integration of resources and ideas.

Without that — nothing scales.

📚 The Good News

The philosophy and design behind LaaS/Homocracy have matured.
Years of refinement.
Conceptual clarity.
Structural coherence.

Now, impact depends on traction.

Your help in spreading these ideas far and wide matters more than you think.

🔁 Our Commitment

No matter the weather.
No matter personal circumstances.
No matter the hurdles.

We will continue crafting materials that promote:

• LaaS / Homocracy
• Its operational vehicle — Laastras

We will keep the 4-week information cycle alive.

⏳ Change Shall Come When:

1️⃣ Policymakers respond to our proposals
OR
2️⃣ Investors find the project sustainable enough to invest

Either way —
We will continue reaching out.
Coldly.
Warmly.
Consistently.

Resistance movements are not an option.
They contradict our ethics and culture.

“May the powers that be, better let us be.”
